violations.text: Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (produce) in the student self-serve refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated eggs during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025
The handwashing sink in the service area was not in service at the time of inspection. Work order has been placed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2024

Chicken that was prepared in advance was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024
Dishes were not allowed to air dry after washing and sanitizing. Staff separated dishes during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2024

The hand-washing sink in the dishwashing room was blocked a trash barrel and fryer oil dispenser. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Ingredients for bibimbap bar such as kimchi and mushrooms were observed held at 48 and 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were moved into refrigeration to chill. New food was placed at bar. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of cook, shrimp was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep with the lid covered. Food was placed onto a sheet pan to cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023

Cheese, Pepperoni, Hummus, and Bread packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels with all required information. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Foods were removed from sale during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2023
